title: Hide SSH key fingerprint from agent status output
description: The agent status command prints the full SSH key fingerprint (SHA256:...) which looks like a secret to users. Daniel Lowd's first reaction was to redact it when sharing a screenshot. The fingerprint is a throwaway identity signing key, not a secret — but printing it causes unnecessary alarm. Should either remove it from default output or hide behind --verbose flag. Ref: bsky thread lowd.cc/3mh4vf3fias2x
